verses.text: Reading the law, they find that Ammonites and Moabites were not to enter God's assembly, and they separate the mixed multitude from Israel. The word read leads at once to obedience. When Scripture shows a duty, a faithful people acts on it without delay.

Reading the law, they find that Ammonites and Moabites were not to enter God's assembly, and they separate the mixed multitude from Israel. The word read leads at once to obedience. When Scripture shows a duty, a faithful people acts on it without delay.

Reading the law, they find that Ammonites and Moabites were not to enter God's assembly, and they separate the mixed multitude from Israel. The word read leads at once to obedience. When Scripture shows a duty, a faithful people acts on it without delay.

But in Nehemiah's absence, Eliashib the priest had given Tobiah, the old enemy, a chamber in the very courts of God's house. Compromise creeps in when the watchman is away. Those who oppose God's work are sometimes welcomed by those who should guard it.

But in Nehemiah's absence, Eliashib the priest had given Tobiah, the old enemy, a chamber in the very courts of God's house. Compromise creeps in when the watchman is away. Those who oppose God's work are sometimes welcomed by those who should guard it.

Nehemiah, recalled to the king, later returns to Jerusalem and learns of the evil Eliashib had done for Tobiah. He comes back to find decay. Reform left untended soon slips back, and the work must be done again.

Nehemiah, recalled to the king, later returns to Jerusalem and learns of the evil Eliashib had done for Tobiah. He comes back to find decay. Reform left untended soon slips back, and the work must be done again.

Grieved, Nehemiah throws all Tobiah's belongings out of the chamber and has the rooms cleansed and the temple vessels and offerings restored. He acts decisively to purge God's house. Holy zeal does not negotiate with what defiles the sanctuary.

Grieved, Nehemiah throws all Tobiah's belongings out of the chamber and has the rooms cleansed and the temple vessels and offerings restored. He acts decisively to purge God's house. Holy zeal does not negotiate with what defiles the sanctuary.

Learning the Levites had gone back to their fields because their portions were withheld, Nehemiah rebukes the rulers, 'Why is the house of God forsaken?' and gathers them again. Neglect of God's servants empties His house. Faithful leaders see that those who minister are not left to starve.

Learning the Levites had gone back to their fields because their portions were withheld, Nehemiah rebukes the rulers, 'Why is the house of God forsaken?' and gathers them again. Neglect of God's servants empties His house. Faithful leaders see that those who minister are not left to starve.

All Judah brings the tithes to the storehouses, and Nehemiah appoints faithful treasurers, praying, 'Remember me, O my God, concerning this.' Order is restored and provision flows. He commits his reforming work to God, content with His remembrance.

All Judah brings the tithes to the storehouses, and Nehemiah appoints faithful treasurers, praying, 'Remember me, O my God, concerning this.' Order is restored and provision flows. He commits his reforming work to God, content with His remembrance.

All Judah brings the tithes to the storehouses, and Nehemiah appoints faithful treasurers, praying, 'Remember me, O my God, concerning this.' Order is restored and provision flows. He commits his reforming work to God, content with His remembrance.

Seeing men tread winepresses and sell goods on the sabbath, Nehemiah rebukes the nobles for profaning the day, as the fathers did before the wrath fell. He guards the sabbath earnestly. To despise God's holy day is to court the judgment that such sin once brought.

Seeing men tread winepresses and sell goods on the sabbath, Nehemiah rebukes the nobles for profaning the day, as the fathers did before the wrath fell. He guards the sabbath earnestly. To despise God's holy day is to court the judgment that such sin once brought.

Seeing men tread winepresses and sell goods on the sabbath, Nehemiah rebukes the nobles for profaning the day, as the fathers did before the wrath fell. He guards the sabbath earnestly. To despise God's holy day is to court the judgment that such sin once brought.

Seeing men tread winepresses and sell goods on the sabbath, Nehemiah rebukes the nobles for profaning the day, as the fathers did before the wrath fell. He guards the sabbath earnestly. To despise God's holy day is to court the judgment that such sin once brought.

He shuts the gates before the sabbath, drives off the merchants who lodge outside, and sets Levites to keep the day holy, praying, 'remember me, O my God.' He fences the sabbath with firm action. Holiness is kept not by wishing but by removing what would break it.

He shuts the gates before the sabbath, drives off the merchants who lodge outside, and sets Levites to keep the day holy, praying, 'remember me, O my God.' He fences the sabbath with firm action. Holiness is kept not by wishing but by removing what would break it.

He shuts the gates before the sabbath, drives off the merchants who lodge outside, and sets Levites to keep the day holy, praying, 'remember me, O my God.' He fences the sabbath with firm action. Holiness is kept not by wishing but by removing what would break it.

He shuts the gates before the sabbath, drives off the merchants who lodge outside, and sets Levites to keep the day holy, praying, 'remember me, O my God.' He fences the sabbath with firm action. Holiness is kept not by wishing but by removing what would break it.

He finds Jews married to women of Ashdod, Ammon, and Moab, whose children cannot even speak the language of Judah. The next generation is being lost. Mixed and ungodly marriages quietly erase a people's faith and very speech.

He finds Jews married to women of Ashdod, Ammon, and Moab, whose children cannot even speak the language of Judah. The next generation is being lost. Mixed and ungodly marriages quietly erase a people's faith and very speech.

Nehemiah contends with them, curses, smites some, and makes them swear to stop, recalling that even Solomon was made to sin by strange wives. He fights the sin with all his strength. If the wisest king fell by this snare, none dare think themselves safe in it.

Nehemiah contends with them, curses, smites some, and makes them swear to stop, recalling that even Solomon was made to sin by strange wives. He fights the sin with all his strength. If the wisest king fell by this snare, none dare think themselves safe in it.

Nehemiah contends with them, curses, smites some, and makes them swear to stop, recalling that even Solomon was made to sin by strange wives. He fights the sin with all his strength. If the wisest king fell by this snare, none dare think themselves safe in it.

He drives away a grandson of the high priest who had married Sanballat's daughter, praying God to remember those who 'defiled the priesthood.' Even high rank is not spared. The nearer to the altar, the less tolerable the corruption.

He drives away a grandson of the high priest who had married Sanballat's daughter, praying God to remember those who 'defiled the priesthood.' Even high rank is not spared. The nearer to the altar, the less tolerable the corruption.

Nehemiah cleanses the people from all foreign defilement, reappoints the priests and Levites to their tasks, and arranges the wood and firstfruits offerings, praying, 'Remember me, O my God, for good.' He ends as he served — seeking God's approval alone. The faithful leave their labour in God's hands, asking only to be remembered for good.
